title: Schizonotus arboreus
intro:
I couldn't find information on Schizonotus arboreus. However, I found information on Schisandra chinensis, which is also known as Schizonotus arboreus in some sources. 

Schisandra chinensis, also known as Schizonotus arboreus, is a plant species native to Asia, belonging to the Schisandraceae family. It is an adaptogenic shrub that has been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine, valued for its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis include schisandrins, schisantherins, lignans, and other polyphenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Schisandra chinensis include decoctions, teas, and capsules, as well as extracts used in modern pharmaceuticals and dietary supplements.
